items.title: Why accelerated resource allocation matters in the age of AI
items.summary: In an era of rapid AI disruption, the winners may not be the companies that predict the future most accurately, but those that learn, reallocate resources, and adapt faster than their competitors.

items.title: Building America’s innovation engine: An interview with David Lebryk
items.summary: The former US Treasury official shares how government can deliver in moments of crisis and how America’s public–private sector dynamic can help fuel its next era of growth.

items.title: A microscope on small businesses: Chile’s productivity opportunity
items.summary: Overcoming scale-related barriers and strengthening ties with other firms—both large and small—can help small businesses in Chile maximize their potential and narrow the productivity gap.

items.title: How Covista is changing education for healthcare professionals
items.summary: Chairman and CEO Steve Beard explains how the organization’s multiyear transformation has reshaped its purpose, structure, and culture.

items.title: A dual transformation agenda for Europe’s chemicals industry
items.summary: The strongest chemical company transformations in Europe perform a difficult balancing act: improving performance while radically reshaping portfolios, operations, and capital allocation.

items.title: The real future of work in healthcare
items.summary: Healthcare is in a productivity crisis that more hiring and more technology alone will not solve. The next era of care depends on making automation work through human–AI workflows.

items.title: How Miro is redefining collaboration across the product development life cycle
items.summary: Miro cofounder and CEO Andrey Khusid reflects on the company’s evolution from a digital whiteboard to an AI-powered workflow platform—and how AI is reshaping competitive advantage in software.

items.title: The serial builder advantage: Why repeat innovators win
items.summary: New McKinsey research explains why organizations that repeatedly build new ventures outperform those that approach innovation as a one-off effort.

items.title: McKinsey Global Publishing’s 2026 book picks
items.summary: We asked McKinsey Global Publishing and Marketing colleagues to share what’s on their bookshelves this year—here’s what they said.

items.title: Lights, camera, algorithm: How AI is rewriting the rules of film and TV
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is reshaping film and TV production from script to screen—but the biggest disruptions may not be the ones anyone is predicting.
